case.title: Vegas Amnesia
case.final_question: What happened last night, and where is Priya's engagement ring?

facts.text: Dev started the night at the Neon Mirage casino bar at 9 PM.
Dev met the Kowalski bachelor party group at the casino bar around 9:30 PM.
Bartender Rosa served Dev and the bachelor party group all evening.
Dev won 8,000 dollars playing blackjack at 11 PM.
Dev bought celebratory drinks for the whole bar at 11:30 PM.
Rosa cut the group off at midnight and called them a cab toward the chapel district.
Dev bought a vintage gold watch at Lucky Lou's Pawn Shop at 1 AM.
Lucky Lou claimed Dev never came into his shop last night.
facts.note: The lie — contradicts f07's receipt. Catching it makes Lou talk.

facts.text: Dev's phone battery died at 1:30 AM, so there are no photos from later in the night.
Dev and best man Marcus held an impromptu ceremony at the Little Neon Chapel at 2 AM.
The chapel ceremony was a novelty friendship ceremony, not a real wedding.
The ring on Dev's finger is a 15 dollar novelty ring from the chapel gift shop.
Dev put Priya's real engagement ring in the hotel room safe at 8:45 PM, before going out.
The hotel safe code is Priya's birthday, 0214.
The group took the rented convertible to the rooftop parking garage at 3 AM.
Dev gave the vintage gold watch to Marcus as a friendship gift at 3:30 AM.
Dev ordered room service at 4 AM: a stack of twelve pancakes.
Chad from the bachelor party crashed on the sofa in Dev's suite.
Dev passed out in the suite at 4:30 AM still wearing the novelty ring.
The remaining blackjack winnings are stuffed inside the ice bucket in Dev's suite.

red_herrings.text: A lipstick-marked napkin in the suite suggests Dev had a romantic encounter.
red_herrings.truth: The napkin came from a bridal shower at the next table; Rosa saw a bridesmaid tuck it into Chad's jacket as a joke.

red_herrings.text: A pawn ticket for a diamond ring was found on the casino floor near Dev's blackjack table.
red_herrings.truth: Lou's ledger shows the ticket belongs to a Ms. Delgado — no connection to Dev.

red_herrings.text: Chad left Dev a voicemail shouting 'congratulations on your wedding!'
red_herrings.truth: Chad was hammered and conflated the friendship ceremony with a wedding.

red_herrings.text: A casino security report says a man in Dev's jacket dove into the lobby fountain at 1 AM.
red_herrings.truth: Dev was at Lucky Lou's at 1 AM (f07); the fountain swimmer was a groomsman who borrowed Dev's jacket.

red_herrings.text: A keycard for room 2412 was in Dev's pocket, hinting he was in someone else's room.
red_herrings.truth: 2412 is Marcus's room; Marcus handed Dev the keycard to hold during the ceremony.

derivable.text: Dev did not actually get married last night; the ceremony was symbolic.
The real engagement ring was never lost — it has been in the hotel safe since before the night began.
Lucky Lou lied about Dev visiting his shop.
Dev's blackjack winnings paid for the watch, and most of the rest is still in the suite.

solution_summary: Dev had a wild but innocent night: won $8,000 at blackjack, bought a vintage watch and gifted it to Marcus, and held a novelty FRIENDSHIP ceremony at the chapel with best man Marcus — not a wedding. The ring on his finger is a $15 gift-shop prop. Priya's real engagement ring — which Dev had taken to Vegas to get resized before the wedding — sat safely in the hotel room safe (code 0214, her birthday) the entire time. The blackjack cash is stuffed in the ice bucket.
